headline: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is 'garbage in, garbage out'
summary: Famed author Margaret Atwood discussed AI at a literary festival. She described the core issue of AI as 'garbage in, garbage out.' Atwood also revealed she has personally experimented with AI tools.

headline: Apple wants permission to buy memory from a blacklisted Chinese supplier
summary: Apple is seeking US approval to buy RAM chips from CXMT, a blacklisted Chinese company. The move aims to alleviate supply chain pressure and rising memory costs. CXMT was previously flagged by the Pentagon for alleged ties to China's military.

headline: The Guardian's Kai Wright refuses to buy a new phone
summary: Journalist Kai Wright, co-host of Stateside, is highlighted for his unique insights. The Peabody Award-winning reporter has hosted several popular programs. A key detail from the interview is his refusal to purchase a new phone.

headline: Indie developers got tired of waiting for a new Star Fox, so they’re making their own
summary: Indie game developers are creating their own Star Fox-inspired title. This project is fueled by nostalgia for classic games like Star Fox 64. The creators aim to deliver a spiritual successor after a long wait for an official new entry.

headline: Steam Machine Scalpers on eBay Are Selling Reservations at Double the Price Valve Is Asking for
summary: Steam Machine reservations have recently opened to the public. Scalpers are already listing these reservations on eBay for over double the retail price. This rapid markup mirrors past issues with high-demand hardware launches.

headline: Warhammer: Age of Sigmar Spearhead City of Ash Review
summary: A new review for 'Warhammer: Age of Sigmar Spearhead City of Ash' has been published. Players can now read an in-depth analysis of the game. The article offers insights into its gameplay and overall experience.

headline: Sony to Delete Movies Owned by PlayStation Users, List Includes More Than 550 Digital Titles
summary: PlayStation has announced it will delete over 550 digital movies from user accounts. The removal is due to the expiration of a licensing agreement with Studio Canal. Affected users have been notified about the upcoming loss of purchased content.

headline: The Best Deals Today: Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake, AirPods Pro 3, Silent Hill f, and More
summary: Discover today's top deals on popular gaming and tech items. Featured offers include the Dragon Quest I & II HD-2D Remake and AirPods Pro 3. The list also highlights discounts on Silent Hill f and various other products.

headline: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
summary: Caracas is currently experiencing what many perceive as Venezuela's most challenging period in modern history. Rescue teams are working tirelessly to find survivors amidst rubble. As hope diminishes, public anger and frustration are intensifying.

headline: Thunderstorms delay hundreds of Heathrow and Gatwick flights
summary: Hundreds of flights at both Heathrow and Gatwick airports have been significantly delayed. Over 900 flights were affected due to severe stormy weather conditions. The disruptions were primarily caused by thunderstorms located south of the UK.

headline: Are we in for a summer of serial heatwaves?
summary: Forecasters are predicting a summer that is likely to be warmer than average. There is an increased chance of experiencing multiple heatwaves throughout the season. Meteorologist Simon King provides an explanation of the summer outlook.

headline: Burnham will need a Moscow test as well as Makerfield test, says ex-military chief
summary: Former Chief of the Defence Staff, Admiral Sir Tony Radakin, stresses the importance of defence for the next Prime Minister. He suggests a 'Moscow test' will be crucial for the incoming leader. This challenge is presented alongside a 'Makerfield test' for Andy Burnham.
